_meta.keyKnowledge: We investigate how composers and performers use music elements, compositional devices and techniques to develop personal voice and achieve specific purposes across cultures and times.

_source.w: We investigate how composers and performers use music elements, compositional devices and techniques to develop personal voice and achieve specific purposes across cultures and times.
y: Investigating how musicians develop personal voice informs our own journey toward artistic individuality.
Understanding how music achieves specific purposes across contexts reveals its extraordinary communicative power.
Cross-cultural and historical study provides the knowledge base for informed and sophisticated music-making.
eg: We research how specific composers developed their personal voice within and beyond established traditions.
We investigate how music serves different social, cultural, spiritual and political purposes across societies.
We analyse how technological developments have shaped compositional and performance practices.
We compare musical traditions from different cultures and examine their distinctive features.
We study the relationship between music, identity and community across different contexts.
We explore how musicians navigate tradition and innovation in developing their artistic practice.
